meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome debugging roadblocks. A high-impact guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code for professional workflows.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about trial and error; it's about systematic isolation. By integrating AI into your Git workflow, you can automate root-cause analysis, generate precise regression tests, and revert broken states instantly. Use Claude Code to audit your commits and Antigravity to simulate edge-case failures before they hit production.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the deadline looms, and your code—once a beacon of logic—is now a labyrinth of silent bugs. Every developer remembers that cold sweat of staring at a failing test suite, paralyzed by the inability to trace a memory leak or a race condition. It is the rite of passage that separates the hobbyists from the engineers.

But here is the secret: modern engineering isn't about knowing everything; it's about orchestrating intelligence. By leveraging Git as your primary debugging canvas and AI as your tactical partner, you turn the 'debugging nightmare' into a verifiable, incremental engineering process. You are about to transform your workflow from reactive chaos to proactive precision.

steps.title: Establish the 'Known-Good' Baseline
steps.description: Before debugging, ensure your Git state is pristine. Use AI to identify the exact commit where the regression began.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the last 5 commits and identify potential logic regressions in the authentication module.

steps.title: Automate Failure Reproduction
steps.description: Don't debug manually. Use AI to craft a unit test that traps the bug, ensuring it never returns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal failing test case for the identified bug in the user-auth service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write test --target auth_service.py --bug-description 'null pointer on session refresh'

steps.title: Context-Aware Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Feed your Git diffs into the AI to explain the 'why' behind the breakage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a causal analysis on this git diff. Why does this change break the asynchronous database call?

steps.title: Iterative Patch Synthesis
steps.description: Generate a fix that respects your existing code style and architectural patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a fix for the identified bug that maintains compatibility with the existing dependency injection container.

steps.title: Verification and Atomic Commit
steps.description: Validate the fix and commit it with a machine-generated, context-rich message.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a conventional commit message for this fix, referencing the issue tracker ID and the nature of the bug.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'fix: resolve race condition in auth refresh' && git push

pro_tips: Always commit your failing test case BEFORE you fix the bug; it validates the fix is real.
Use 'git stash' to isolate your experimental AI-generated fixes from your current working branch.
Configure Claude Code to ignore node_modules or build artifacts to speed up context analysis.
Leverage Git hooks to run your AI-generated unit tests automatically on every 'git commit'.
Treat your Git history as a documentation log; use AI to summarize your learning milestones in every merge request.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, start contributing to an open-source project to test your new debugging speed in a collaborative, high-pressure environment.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical precision to master any codebase. Stop fighting the machine and start mastering the build process. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
